bird.summary: The marsh antwren (Formicivora acutirostris) is an insectivorous bird in subfamily Thamnophilinae of family Thamnophilidae, the "typical an…

relations.subject: Marsh antwren
relations.evidence: As its name implies, the marsh antwren primarily inhabits coastal and riverine marshes; it occurs less frequently in flooded plains and in the transition zone between mangroves and drier land
relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

relations.subject: Marsh antwren
relations.evidence: Some patches of habitat, including the type-locality, are under constant human pressure and have been reduced by fires, allotments and landfills." Conversion of habitat for other uses, pollution, and the effects of climate change are also threats
relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。
